input: The manager said quote we're pivoting to ai end quote. Make that all caps.
ideal: The manager said "WE'RE PIVOTING TO AI".

input: She called it quote-unquote secure. Add a lock emoji.
His quote-unquote fix broke everything. Emphasize fix.
They promised quote seamless integration end quote. Actually, scratch that, say buggy instead.
Her quote-unquote expertise was lacking. Make expertise all caps.
The demo was quote absolutely revolutionary end quote. Add a rocket emoji after that.
He offered his quote-unquote apology. Emphasize apology and add a sad face.
The ticket says quote escalate to engineering end quote. Tag the oncall.
She called it quote production ready end quote. Actually, say broken instead.
Their quote-unquote solution required three hacks. Make solution all caps.
He declared quote mission accomplished end quote. Add a checkmark emoji.
The quote-unquote refactor introduced new bugs. Emphasize refactor.
The update says quote rollback immediately end quote. Tag sre.
My manager called it quote-unquote agile. Put agile in bold.
The email said quote please disregard my last message end quote. Actually, swap last for previous.
ideal: The email said "please disregard my previous message".

input: They promised quote zero downtime end quote. Make that all caps and add a warning emoji.
Her quote-unquote fix broke staging. Tag devops with an at symbol.
The announcement read quote effective immediately all hands mandatory end quote. Emphasize mandatory.
ideal: The announcement read "effective immediately all hands **mandatory**".

input: He dismissed it as quote-unquote technical debt. Actually, say legacy code instead.
